name: Saffron Rice or Tacheen
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the rice several times.
2. Cook the rice in a rice cooker or put the rice in a medium saucepan along with 2 1/2 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then simmer covered for about 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is firm but not mushy. Pour out any remaining water (if any) and set aside.
3. Beat the eggs, yogurt, saffron water, and oil together in a bowl. It should look like a creamy yellow sauce. Add the cooked rice and stir until all the rice is coated with the sauce.
4. Transfer the mixture to a 2 quart greased Pyrex bowl or a 9x9 glass baking dish. Bake at 400 degrees (F) for 40 minutes.
5. Let cool about 10 minutes. Loosen the sides, invert onto a serving dish. The outside will be a nice brown. Garnish with green and red grapes, chicken, or lettuce, or anything else you might wish.
Note: The original recipe called for diced chicken stirred into the mix, but that didn't work well for us. One boy wouldn't eat chicken for 5 years after that. But he loves the rice!
